questions.question: Which metric is most useful when false negatives are very costly (e.g. disease detection)?
questions.explanation: Recall = TP/(TP+FN). Maximising recall minimises missed positives — critical in medical diagnosis.

questions.question: Logistic regression outputs:
questions.explanation: Logistic regression uses the sigmoid function to map any input to a probability between 0 and 1.

questions.question: AUC-ROC close to 0.5 means:
options: Model performs no better than random guessing
questions.explanation: AUC=0.5 means the ROC curve follows the diagonal — equivalent to random guessing.

questions.question: Which algorithm is most interpretable for classification?
questions.explanation: A decision tree can be visualised and its decisions traced — making it the most interpretable option.

questions.question: When classes are highly imbalanced, accuracy is misleading because:
options: A model predicting only the majority class can score very high
It doesn't account for training time
questions.explanation: With 95% negative class: a model that always predicts negative gets 95% accuracy without learning anything useful.

questions.question: Precision is defined as:
questions.explanation: Precision = TP/(TP+FP). It measures: of all positive predictions, how many were actually correct?

questions.question: One-vs-Rest (OvR) is a strategy for:
questions.explanation: OvR trains one binary classifier per class (vs. all others), enabling multi-class classification.

questions.question: K-Nearest Neighbours classification predicts based on:
options: The majority class among the K nearest training points
questions.explanation: KNN is instance-based — it finds the K closest training examples and returns their majority class.

questions.question: Cross-entropy loss is commonly used for:
questions.explanation: Cross-entropy measures the divergence between predicted probabilities and true class labels.

questions.question: The confusion matrix diagonal represents:
questions.explanation: The main diagonal of a confusion matrix holds correctly classified counts for each class.
